items.title: [正面] YES. This is actually FUN, unlike most Gacha games...
items.summary:
YES. This is actually FUN, unlike most Gacha games that are a hefty slog. The rates are pretty high too (or maybe it's just my luck) but I got the banner characters in a single 5-pull. *smug*
Anyway, this is undeniably a gacha game sure, but it's such a good game concept wise too.

Inspired by roguelike deckbuilders, but I'd say it's more like a deck-empower-er? See, basically your character doesn't build the deck; they all come with a default set of 5 cards: the strike card, defense card, 2 personalized cards, and 1 awakener card you unlock mid-dungeon.

Instead of unlocking new cards, every run through a dungeon gives you "Orisons", which are randomized power ups you get to apply to a card: for example, an Orison to apply a defense buff that you can stack on your defense card (for more defense) or maybe apply it to a strike card (for a defensive attack!). There's a lot of subtle strategy that comes to mixing and matching orisons to your cards each run.

At shops you can also trim your deck removing cards you don't use, (like say, in my case I often sell my tanks Strike card.) and in exchange you get black sigils (consider black sigils this game's currency), and you can use those black sigils to buy relics (a passive powerup).

If you lose to a boss, you can use a resource (recharges once a day: you have 5/5) to revive, buuuuuut you could also not use that and just retry the fight at no cost. Maybe your tactic was just a little off, but you feel like you got close enough? No need to redo the entire dungeon.

Speaking of dungeons, dungeons are little nodes where your character travels through and with a bit of wit and cunning you should be able to make it through trap-less...
On a dungeon you'll find blank nodes to pass through, cracked nodes that are a 1-way, tunnels and passages that warp you around, enemies, traps, shops, healing stations, and probably some more I can't think to write right now, but it all leads to a confrontation with a biiiig enemy. The nodes themselves are also an aspect of the gameplay you have to consider carefully, as they healing stations mentioned are alternatively, a place where you can awaken your characters to get their 5th card. Do you take the power at the sacrifice of healing precious HP? 

Anyway, I can go at this for hours, but what I'm trying to say is: this game has a fun intricate system in it you can learn if you give it time. It's well worth the experience. I've actually beaten enemies 10 levels ahead of my "recommended" because of how tactically I've been playing to minimize having to grind. c;

Now taking a step away from gameplay real brief: the music is not a lot but it's plenty relaxing and haunting, as is the art-style. So if you're into gacha games for the appeal and atmosphere, this might be a night change of pace for something more somber instead of some genshi clone. The themes follow the Cthulhu mythos, so there's lots of eldritch creatures and monsters; heck, I named my character "Tawil" the moment I heard silver keys were mentioned. (If you know Lovecraftian mythos you might get it! XD)

Soooo anyway, it's a free game, no harm in trying it out! Have fun!
